intro: A quiet holiday news cycle: the sharpest story of the day is on-chain, not in a keynote — bitcoin's supply has flipped to majority-loss for the first time this cycle.

items.headline: GitHub Copilot CLI drops personal access token requirement in Actions
items.summary: GitHub Copilot CLI can now authenticate inside GitHub Actions using the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN, removing the need to provision and manage a personal access token for CI workflows.
items.why: Cuts a common setup and security headache for teams wiring Copilot's CLI agent into CI pipelines.

items.headline: GitHub lets enterprises default Copilot to automatic model selection
items.summary: Enterprise administrators can now set Copilot's model choice to "auto" by default across an organization via managed-settings.json, letting Copilot pick the best model per request instead of a fixed one.
items.why: Signals GitHub pushing model-routing-by-default as the norm for large Copilot deployments, likely affecting cost and latency.

items.headline: Anthropic ships Claude Sonnet 5, a cheaper agentic default model
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Sonnet 5, its most agentic mid-size model yet, now the default for Free and Pro plans and priced at an introductory $2/$10 per million input/output tokens through August 31.
items.why: A materially cheaper model that approaches Opus-class agentic performance changes the cost math for anyone running Claude-based agents at scale.

items.headline: Anthropic confirms export controls lifted on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.summary: Anthropic said the U.S. Commerce Department has lifted the export-control order that had taken its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models offline for nearly three weeks, restoring access across Anthropic's products.
items.why: Ends a standoff that briefly pulled Anthropic's most capable models from the market over national-security concerns.

items.headline: Europe's regulators warn AI is moving faster than financial rules can follow
items.summary: Speaking at the ECB Forum, UK FCA chief Nikhil Rathi and ECB president Christine Lagarde said traditional rulemaking cycles can't keep pace with agentic AI, and floated market-wide "kill switch" style safeguards.
items.why: A concrete signal that circuit-breaker-style AI safeguards for financial markets are moving from theory to policy discussion.

items.headline: GitHub Models will be fully retired July 30, 2026
items.summary: GitHub confirmed that GitHub Models — including its playground, model catalog, inference API, and bring-your-own-key support — will be shut down for all customers at the end of the month.
items.why: Teams prototyping against GitHub's hosted model catalog have under a month to migrate before it disappears.

items.headline: GitHub Issue Fields reaches general availability
items.summary: Structured, typed custom fields for GitHub Issues — for tracking priority, effort, dates, and other metadata — are now generally available across Free, Team, Enterprise, and data-residency plans.
items.why: Brings GitHub's native issue tracking closer to feature parity with dedicated project-management tools.

items.headline: GitHub adds free public-repo secret scanning monitoring for enterprises
items.summary: GitHub Secret Protection customers can now enable public monitoring in preview at no extra cost, alerting enterprises when their secrets appear in public repositories anywhere on GitHub.
items.why: Closes a common leak vector — credentials pushed to public forks or personal repos outside the org's own scanning scope.

items.headline: More bitcoin is now held at a loss than at a profit, in a first for this cycle
items.summary: Roughly 10.83 million BTC are sitting at an unrealized loss versus 9.22 million in profit, the first time loss-making supply has overtaken profitable supply since the current cycle began — a crossover that has historically marked bear-market bottoms.
items.why: This on-chain metric has flagged every major cycle bottom to date, making today's flip the most-watched bitcoin signal of the year so far.

items.headline: Bitcoin whales bought $16.7 billion even as ETFs bled a record $4 billion
items.summary: Large holders added more than 270,000 BTC over two weeks while U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs suffered their worst month on record in June, a divergence between whale accumulation and institutional outflows that has preceded past cycle lows.
items.why: Large, high-conviction buyers stepping in while institutions retreat is a pattern traders watch closely for bottom formation.

items.headline: Bitcoin ETFs post $221 million inflow, snapping a 10-day outflow streak
items.summary: U.S.-listed spot bitcoin ETFs took in $221 million on Thursday, their strongest single-day intake in two months, ending ten straight days of net redemptions.
items.why: First tentative sign that the institutional selling pressure of the past month may be easing.
